entries.name: Quantum mechanics
entries.definition: Fundamental theory describing the behavior of matter and energy at the quantum scale, including wave-particle duality, uncertainty principle, and quantum states.
correspondences: Observer effect suggests consciousness affects reality
Quantum superposition reflects multiple possibilities existing simultaneously

entries.name: Quantum entanglement
entries.definition: Phenomenon where particles become correlated in such a way that the quantum state of each particle cannot be described independently, even when separated by large distances.
correspondences: Demonstrates the fundamental interconnectedness of all things
Suggests non-local consciousness and instant communication
Parallels mystical teachings about unity and oneness

entries.name: Observer effect (physics)
entries.definition: The fact that simply observing or measuring a quantum system will disturb it, changing the measurement results.
correspondences: Consciousness as an active participant in reality creation
Attention and awareness as fundamental forces
Parallels meditation teachings about witness consciousness

entries.name: Wave-particle duality
entries.definition: The concept that every particle or quantum entity can be described as either a particle or a wave, but not both simultaneously.
correspondences: Dual nature of reality - material and energetic

entries.name: Quantum consciousness
entries.definition: Theoretical framework suggesting that quantum mechanical phenomena are relevant to consciousness and cognitive processes.
correspondences: Consciousness as fundamental aspect of reality

entries.name: Copenhagen interpretation
entries.definition: The standard interpretation of quantum mechanics emphasizing the role of measurement and the probabilistic nature of quantum phenomena.
correspondences: Role of measurement in creating definite outcomes
Uncertainty as fundamental feature of existence
